name: Thomson Reuters ProLaw Legal Practice Management API
what_it_does: Thomson Reuters ProLaw legal practice management REST API for small to mid-size law firm billing, matter management, time entry, document management, and trust accounting. Enables AI agents to manage client and matter setup for automated matter intake workflow, handle attorney time entry capture and billing narrative for automated time recording, access prebilling review and invoice generation for billing cycle automation, retrieve trust accounting and client funds management for IOLTA compliance automation, manage conflict of interest checking for new matter intake, handle accounts receivable and collections workflow for law firm cash flow automation, access document assembly and court deadline calendar for litigation workflow automation, retrieve profitability and realization metrics for law firm business intelligence, manage contact and relationship data for CRM integration, and integrate ProLaw with Microsoft Office, document management, and e-billing platforms.
use_cases: Capturing attorney time from productivity agents
Processing invoices from billing automation agents
Managing trust accounts from IOLTA compliance agents
Checking conflicts from matter intake agents
not_for: Am Law 100 firms requiring enterprise scale (use Elite 3E or Aderant)
Courts and case management for judicial systems
Legal e-discovery and document review platforms
best_when: A small to mid-size law firm (5-100 attorneys) wanting AI agents to automate billing, matter management, time capture, conflict checking, and trust accounting within the Thomson Reuters ProLaw practice management system.
avoid_when: IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING STATE BAR COMPLIANCE: Automated trust account transactions via ProLaw must comply with state bar IOLTA rules including separate ledger, monthly reconciliation, and attorney authorization for disbursement; automated trust disbursement without attorney approval creates state bar disciplinary and malpractice exposure. TIME ENTRY CERTIFICATION BEFORE BILLING: AI-generated time entries via ProLaw API must be reviewed and certified by billing attorney before invoice generation; automated billing of uncertified AI time entries creates ABA Model Rule 1.5 fee dispute risk. CONFLICT CHECK DATA CURRENCY: Automated ProLaw conflict check is bounded by matter and adverse party data entered at time of check; automated conflict clearance without supplemental search for new business with lateral hires or recently added parties creates malpractice exposure. Attorney-client privilege in billing narratives — automated billing narrative generation must not include privileged communication detail; overly specific narratives referencing confidential communications may waive privilege in fee dispute proceedings.

auth.notes: Thomson Reuters ProLaw uses API key authentication. REST API with XML/JSON. New York City HQ (Thomson Reuters Corporation, NYSE: TRI / TSX: TRI). ProLaw originally developed by ProLaw Software Inc.; acquired by Thomson Reuters. Mid-market law firm target: 5-100 attorneys. Integrated billing, matter management, document management, and trust accounting. Competes with Clio, MyCase, and PracticePanther for small/mid law firm practice management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user annual subscription with implementation services
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user; cloud-hosted and on-premise options; implementation professional services
pricing.notes: New York NY. Thomson Reuters NYSE/TSX:TRI. Mid-market law firm focus: 5-100 attorneys. Per-user annual subscription. Implementation professional services typically required.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; billing and financial API calls throttled for data integrity

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — BILLING EVENTS REQUIRE POLLING: ProLaw does not support native webhooks; invoice approval, payment posting, and trust transactions require scheduled polling; implement time-based polling aligned with billing cycle dates

IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING ATTORNEY APPROVAL GATE: Automated trust account entries via ProLaw API must require explicit attorney authorization before posting; automated trust fund movements without attorney approval create state bar IOLTA rule violation and potential client funds misappropriation

AI TIME ENTRY ATTORNEY CERTIFICATION REQUIRED: Time entries created via ProLaw API by AI agents must be queued for attorney review and certification before prebill generation; automated prebilling of AI-generated entries without attorney certification violates ABA billing rules and creates fee dispute exposure

On-premise deployment network access dependency — ProLaw on-premise installations require agent network connectivity to law firm ProLaw server; cloud-based AI agents need VPN or secure tunnel; cloud-hosted ProLaw SaaS avoids this but requires separate cloud deployment selection

Conflict check completeness for new matter intake — automated conflict check via ProLaw API searches matter and contact records loaded at time of check; new adverse parties or lateral attorney conflicts may not be loaded; supplement automated ProLaw conflict check with manual review for complex matters

Integration with Microsoft Office automation — ProLaw tight integration with Microsoft Office (Word, Outlook) for document assembly and email capture is largely UI-based; automated document generation and email matter linking requires Office COM automation or Office 365 API separate from ProLaw REST API
agent_readiness.security_notes: Legal practice management. SOC2. API key. US. Attorney billing, client matter, and IOLTA trust account data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Legal practice management. SOC2. API key. US. Attorney billing and trust account data.
